decision: Contained auth login and analytics-adjacent bet flows behind persistence-neutral boundary types.
reason: Acceptance criteria require remaining legacy-model usage to be removed or pushed to repository/edge adapters within analytics, auth login, and adjacent boundary consumers.
impact: Production code no longer imports models in auth login, and scoped bet math adapters now map only explicit boundary fields.

decision: Accepted repository-edge containment for remaining legacy model usage in analytics-adjacent flows.
reason: Repositories remain the explicit persistence adapters; the task only requires removing or tightly containing legacy models from analytics, auth login, and touched boundary consumers.
impact: Auth login now maps from a local DB row into boundary.AuthenticatedUser, while analytics and handler test seams consume boundary bet/user/market shapes outside repository edges.
